subjects.name: Basic Electrical & Electronics Engineering

units.title: Part A -- DC & AC Circuits
topics: Electrical circuit elements (R, L, C), Ohm's law and its limitations, KCL and KVL
Series, parallel and series-parallel circuits, superposition theorem, simple numerical problems
AC fundamentals: equation of AC voltage/current, waveform, time period, frequency, amplitude, phase
Voltage-current phasor relationships in R, L, C circuits; impedance, active/reactive/apparent power, power factor

units.title: Part A -- Machines and Measuring Instruments
topics: Construction, principle and operation of DC motor, DC generator, single-phase transformer, three-phase induction motor, and alternator
Applications of electrical machines
Construction and working of PMMC and moving-iron (MI) instruments, and wattmeter

units.title: Part A -- Energy Resources, Electricity Bill & Safety Measures
topics: Conventional and non-conventional energy resources; hydel, nuclear, solar and wind power generation layouts
Power rating of household appliances, electricity tariff and bill calculation for domestic consumers
Equipment safety: fuse working principle, electric shock and earthing, safety precautions

units.title: Part B -- Semiconductor Devices
topics: Evolution of electronics -- vacuum tubes to nanoelectronics
PN junction diode characteristics, Zener effect and Zener diode characteristics
Bipolar junction transistor -- CB, CE, CC configurations and characteristics; elementary small-signal CE amplifier

units.title: Part B -- Basic Electronic Circuits and Instrumentation
topics: Rectifiers and power supplies: block diagram of a DC power supply, full-wave bridge rectifier, capacitor filter, Zener voltage regulator
Amplifiers: block diagram of a public address system; CE (RC-coupled) amplifier circuit and frequency response
Electronic instrumentation: block diagram of an electronic instrumentation system

units.title: Part B -- Digital Electronics
topics: Number systems, logic gates including universal gates, BCD, excess-3, Gray and Hamming codes
Boolean algebra -- basic theorems and properties, truth tables and functionality of logic gates
Simple combinational circuits -- half and full adders
Introduction to sequential circuits -- flip-flops, registers and counters (elementary treatment)

course_outcomes: Describe fundamental laws and operating principles of motors/generators and MC/MI instruments
Demonstrate the working of electrical machines, measuring instruments and power generation stations
Apply mathematical tools and fundamental concepts to derive equations related to electrical circuits and machines
Calculate electrical load and electricity bill of residential and commercial buildings

seo.title: Basic Electrical & Electronics Engineering - 1-1
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 1-1 Basic Electrical & Electronics Engineering: DC/AC circuits, machines, energy resources, semiconductor devices and digital electronics...

subjects.name: Chemistry

units.title: Structure and Bonding Models
topics: Fundamentals of quantum mechanics, Schrodinger wave equation, significance of psi and psi-squared
Particle in a one-dimensional box
Molecular orbital theory -- bonding in homo- and hetero-nuclear diatomic molecules, energy level diagrams of O2 and CO
Pi-molecular orbitals of butadiene and benzene, calculation of bond order

units.title: Modern Engineering Materials
topics: Semiconductors -- introduction, basic concepts, applications
Superconductors -- introduction, basic concepts, applications
Supercapacitors -- introduction, basic concepts, classification, applications
Nanomaterials -- introduction, classification, properties and applications of fullerenes, carbon nanotubes and graphene nanoparticles

units.title: Electrochemistry and Applications
topics: Electrochemical cell, Nernst equation, cell potential calculations
Potentiometry -- potentiometric titrations (redox); conductivity, conductometric titrations (acid-base)
Construction and working of standard hydrogen electrode and calomel electrode
Primary cells (zinc-air battery), secondary cells (lithium-ion batteries) -- working and cell reactions
Fuel cells, hydrogen-oxygen fuel cell, polymer electrolyte membrane fuel cells (PEMFC)

units.title: Polymer Chemistry
topics: Introduction to polymers, functionality of monomers, chain growth and step growth polymerization
Coordination polymerization with examples and mechanisms
Thermoplastics and thermosetting plastics -- PVC, Teflon, Bakelite, Nylon-6,6, carbon fibres
Elastomers -- Buna-S, Buna-N: preparation, properties and applications
Conducting polymers -- polyacetylene, polyaniline; biodegradable polymers -- PGA, PLA

units.title: Instrumental Methods and Applications
topics: Electromagnetic spectrum, absorption of radiation, Beer-Lambert law -- applications and limitations
UV-visible spectroscopy -- electronic transitions and instrumentation
IR spectroscopy -- instrumentation, identification and applications
Procedure and applications of MRI and CT scans

course_outcomes: Understand the concepts of molecular orbitals and bond orders
Apply the principle of band diagrams in the application of conductors and semiconductors
Compare the materials of construction for battery and fuel cells
Explain the preparation, properties, and applications of thermoplastics, thermosetting polymers, elastomers, and conducting polymers
Summarize the concepts of instrumental methods and explain the principles of spectrometry

seo.title: Chemistry - 1-1
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 CSE/EEE 1-1 Chemistry notes: quantum structure and bonding, modern engineering materials, electrochemistry, polymer chemistry, and instrumental...

subjects.name: Chemistry Lab

units.title: Laboratory Experiments
topics: Determination of crystal-field splitting energy (10Dq) by spectrophotometric method
Conductometric titration of a strong acid against a strong base
Conductometric titration of a weak acid against a strong base
Determination of cell constant and conductance of solutions
Potentiometric determination and redox titration experiments
Determination of acid concentration in a lead-acid battery
Preparation of Bakelite
Verification of Beer-Lambert law using a colorimeter or UV-visible spectrophotometer
Identification of functional groups using infrared spectroscopy
Preparation of nanomaterials by precipitation method
Determination of ferrous iron by dichrometry
Determination of total hardness of water by EDTA method
Estimation of vitamin C
Determination of magnesium in an antacid

course_outcomes: Determine the cell constant and conductance of solutions
Prepare advanced polymer Bakelite materials
Measure the strength of an acid present in secondary batteries
Analyse the IR spectra of some organic compounds
Calculate the strength of acid in a Pb-acid battery

seo.title: Chemistry Lab - 1-1
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 CSE/EEE 1-1 Chemistry Lab: conductometric and potentiometric titrations, Bakelite preparation, UV-Visible and IR spectroscopy experiments.

subjects.name: Communicative English

units.title: Human Values -- Gift of Magi
topics: Listening: identifying topic, context and specific information from short audio texts
Speaking: asking/answering general questions on familiar topics; introducing oneself and others
Reading: skimming for main idea, scanning for specific information
Writing: mechanics of writing -- capitalization, spelling, punctuation, parts of sentences
Grammar: parts of speech, basic sentence structures, forming questions
Vocabulary: synonyms, antonyms, affixes (prefixes/suffixes), root words

units.title: Nature -- The Brook by Alfred Tennyson
topics: Listening: answering questions on main and supporting ideas
Speaking: pair/small-group discussion followed by short structured talks
Reading: identifying sequence of ideas and verbal linking techniques
Writing: structure of a paragraph; paragraph writing on specific topics
Grammar: cohesive devices -- linkers, articles, zero article, prepositions
Vocabulary: homonyms, homophones, homographs

units.title: Biography -- Elon Musk
topics: Listening: global comprehension and summarizing
Speaking: discussing specific topics in pairs/small groups and reporting back
Reading: detailed reading with inference and context-clue strategies
Writing: summarizing, note-making, paraphrasing
Grammar: verb tenses, subject-verb agreement, compound words, collocations

units.title: Inspiration -- The Toys of Peace by Saki
topics: Listening: making predictions with and without video
Speaking: role plays for formal/informal conversational English -- asking for and giving information/directions
Reading: using graphic elements in texts to convey information and trends
Writing: letter writing -- official letters, resumes
Grammar: reporting verbs, direct and indirect speech, active and passive voice
Vocabulary: words often confused, jargon

units.title: Motivation -- The Power of Intrapersonal Communication
topics: Listening: identifying key terms and answering comprehension questions
Speaking: formal oral presentations on academic topics
Reading: reading comprehension
Writing: structured essays on specific topics
Grammar: editing short texts -- articles, prepositions, tenses, subject-verb agreement

course_outcomes: Understand the context, topic, and pieces of specific information from social or transactional dialogues
Apply grammatical structures to formulate sentences and correct word forms
Analyze discourse markers to speak clearly on a specific topic in informal discussions and enhance vocabulary
Evaluate reading/listening texts and write summaries based on global comprehension of these texts
Create a coherent paragraph, essay, and resume

seo.title: Communicative English - 1-1
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 1-1 Communicative English notes covering listening, speaking, reading, writing, grammar, and vocabulary across five thematic units.

units.title: Language and Professional Communication Activities
topics: English speech sounds: vowels, consonants and pronunciation practice
Accent neutralization, word stress, rhythm and intonation
Everyday communication and just-a-minute speaking
Role plays for social and professional situations
Email writing and netiquette
Resume, cover-letter and statement-of-purpose preparation
Group discussions and collaborative communication
Debates and structured argument
PowerPoint and poster presentations

subjects.name: Computer Programming Lab

units.title: Programming Laboratory Exercises
topics: Familiarization with the programming environment and basic problem solving
Algorithms and flowcharts for simple computational problems
Programs using arithmetic expressions and operators
Operator precedence, type conversion and formatted input/output
Programs using conditional statements
Programs using iterative statements
One-dimensional arrays and searching techniques
Matrices, strings and bubble sort
Pointers, structures and dynamic memory allocation
Bit fields and linked-list operations
User-defined functions and numerical applications such as Euler's method
Recursive functions
Call by reference and pointer-based argument passing
Text and binary file operations

course_outcomes: Read, understand, and trace the execution of programs written in C
Select the right control structure for solving a problem
Develop C programs that utilize memory efficiently using constructs like pointers
Develop, debug and execute programs demonstrating arrays, functions and pointer basics

seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 1-1 Computer Programming Lab: 14-week C programming lab covering control structures, arrays, pointers, structures, recursion and file handling.

subjects.name: Electrical & Electronics Engineering Workshop

units.title: Tools, Instruments and Components
topics: Identification and safe use of common electrical and electronic workshop tools
Use of voltmeters, ammeters, multimeters, LCR-Q meters, power supplies, CROs, DSOs, function generators and frequency counters
Identification, colour coding, testing and comparison of resistors, capacitors, inductors, diodes, transistors and integrated circuits

units.title: Electrical Engineering Experiments
topics: Demonstration of basic electrical equipment
Verification of Kirchhoff's current and voltage laws
Verification of the superposition theorem
Magnetization characteristics of a DC shunt generator
Measurement of power and power factor using a single-phase wattmeter
Measurement of earth resistance using a megger
Calculation of electrical energy for domestic premises

units.title: Electronics Engineering Experiments
topics: Forward- and reverse-bias V-I characteristics of a PN-junction diode
V-I characteristics of a Zener diode and its use as a voltage regulator
Implementation of half-wave and full-wave rectifiers
Verification of AND, OR and NOT gates using integrated circuits
Verification of XOR and XNOR gates using integrated circuits
Verification of universal gates using integrated circuits
Implementation of Boolean functions using logic gates

course_outcomes: Measure voltage, current and power in an electrical circuit
Measure resistance using a Wheatstone bridge
Investigate critical field resistance and critical speed of a DC shunt generator
Investigate reactive power and power factor in electrical loads
Identify and test common electronic components and use electronic measuring instruments
Plot characteristics of semiconductor devices and implement basic digital circuits

seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 1-1 Electrical & Electronics Engineering Workshop: hands-on practice on electrical wiring, electronic devices and circuit fundamentals.

subjects.name: Engineering Graphics

units.title: Introduction, Curves and Scales
topics: Lines, lettering and dimensioning, geometrical constructions, constructing regular polygons
Construction of ellipse, parabola and hyperbola; cycloids, involutes, normal and tangent to curves
Plain scales, diagonal scales and vernier scales

units.title: Orthographic Projections
topics: Reference planes, projections of a point in any of the four quadrants
Projections of straight lines parallel/perpendicular/inclined to reference planes
Projections of planes -- perpendicular, parallel and inclined to reference planes

units.title: Projections of Solids
topics: Types of solids: polyhedra and solids of revolution
Projections of solids in simple positions -- axis perpendicular/parallel to reference planes
Projection of solids with axis inclined to one reference plane

units.title: Sections and Development of Surfaces
topics: Perpendicular and inclined section planes, sectional views and true shape of section
Development of surfaces -- parallel line and radial line development
Development of a cube, prism, cylinder, pyramid and cone

units.title: Conversion of Views and Computer Graphics
topics: Conversion of isometric views to orthographic views and vice versa
Creating 2D/3D drawings including PCB layouts and transformations using AutoCAD (not for end examination)

course_outcomes: Understand the principles of engineering drawing including curves, scales, and orthographic/isometric projections
Draw and interpret orthographic projections of points, lines, planes and solids
Understand and draw projections of solids in various positions in the first quadrant
Explain principles behind development of surfaces
Prepare isometric and perspective sections of simple solids

seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 1-1 Engineering Graphics notes: curves and scales, orthographic projections, projections of solids, development of surfaces, and AutoCAD basics.

subjects.name: Introduction to Programming

units.title: Introduction to Programming and Problem Solving
topics: History of computers, basic organization of a computer, ALU, I/O units, memory, program counter
Basics of a computer program -- algorithms, flowcharts, pseudocode
Problem-solving strategies: top-down, bottom-up, time and space complexity
Introduction to compilation and execution, primitive data types, variables, constants
Basic input/output, operations, type conversion and casting

units.title: Control Structures
topics: Simple sequential programs
Conditional statements (if, if-else, switch)
Loops (for, while, do-while), break and continue

units.title: Arrays and Strings
topics: Array indexing, memory model, programs with arrays of integers
Two-dimensional arrays
Introduction to strings

units.title: Pointers and User-Defined Data Types
topics: Pointers, types of pointers, dereferencing and address operators
Pointer and address arithmetic, array manipulation using pointers
User-defined data types -- structures and unions

units.title: Functions and File Handling
topics: Function declaration, definition, call, return types and arguments
Modifying parameters using pointers, arrays as parameters, recursive functions
Storage classes, scope and lifetime of variables, memory management
Basics of file handling

course_outcomes: Understand basics of computers, algorithms and algorithmic thinking
Analyse a problem and develop an algorithm to solve it
Implement various algorithms using the C programming language
Understand more advanced features of the C language
Develop problem-solving skills and the ability to debug and optimize code

seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 1-1 Introduction to Programming notes: computing fundamentals, control structures, arrays/strings, pointers, functions and file handling in C.

units.title: AI Tools
topics: Experiment with prompt engineering and compare model responses
Use a generative AI assistant for creative-writing exercises

course_outcomes: Perform basic computer-hardware troubleshooting
Explain computer hardware components and their interdependencies
Safeguard computer systems from viruses and worms
Prepare documents and presentations using productivity tools

seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 1-1 IT Workshop: PC hardware/software assembly, OS installation, LAN connectivity, and productivity tool practice.

subjects.name: Linear Algebra and Calculus

units.title: Matrices
topics: Linear transformations and matrices; rank of a matrix by echelon form and normal form
Diagonalization of a matrix, Cauchy-Binet formulae (without proof)
Inverse of non-singular matrices by Gauss-Jordan method
System of linear equations: Gauss elimination, Jacobi and Gauss-Seidel iteration methods
Applications such as electrical circuit problems

units.title: Eigenvalues, Eigenvectors and Orthogonal Transformation
topics: Eigenvalues, eigenvectors and their properties, Cayley-Hamilton theorem (without proof)
Finding inverse and power of a matrix by Cayley-Hamilton theorem
Quadratic forms and nature of quadratic forms
Reduction of quadratic form to canonical form by orthogonal transformation

units.title: Mean Value Theorems
topics: Rolle's theorem, Lagrange's mean value theorem with geometrical interpretation
Cauchy's mean value theorem, Taylor's and Maclaurin's theorems with remainders (without proof)
Problems and applications on the above theorems

units.title: Partial Differentiation and Applications
topics: Functions of several variables: continuity, differentiability, partial derivatives, total derivatives, chain rule
Homogeneous functions and Euler's theorem
Taylor's and Maclaurin's series expansion of functions of two variables
Jacobian, functional dependence, maxima and minima of functions of two variables, Lagrange multipliers

units.title: Multiple Integrals
topics: Double integrals, triple integrals, change of order of integration
Change of variables to polar, spherical and cylindrical coordinates
Finding areas (by double integrals) and volumes (by double and triple integrals)

course_outcomes: Develop and use matrix algebra techniques needed by engineers for practical applications
Utilize mean value theorems for geometrical interpretation
Work with functions of several variables, including maxima and minima
Apply important tools of calculus in higher dimensions
Evaluate double and triple integrals in Cartesian, polar, spherical and cylindrical coordinates

seo.title: Linear Algebra and Calculus - 1-1
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 1-1 Linear Algebra and Calculus notes: matrices, eigenvalues, mean value theorems, partial differentiation, and multiple integrals.

subjects.name: Basic Civil and Mechanical Engineering

units.title: Part A -- Basics of Civil Engineering
topics: Role of civil engineers in society, disciplines of civil engineering (structural, geotechnical, transportation, hydraulics/water resources, environmental)
Building construction and planning; construction materials -- cement, aggregate, bricks, cement concrete, steel
Introduction to prefabricated construction techniques, glossary of civil terms, significance of the NBC

units.title: Part A -- Surveying
topics: Objectives of surveying, horizontal and angular measurements, introduction to bearings
Levelling instruments, simple problems on levelling and bearings, contour mapping

units.title: Part A -- Transportation, Water Resources and Environmental Engineering
topics: Importance of transportation in economic development, flexible and rigid highway pavements, road safety basics
Basics of harbour, tunnel, airport and railway engineering
Sources and quality of water, introduction to hydrology, rainwater harvesting, water storage and conveyance structures

units.title: Part B -- Introduction to Mechanical Engineering
topics: Role of mechanical engineering in industry and society across energy, manufacturing, automotive, aerospace and marine sectors
Engineering materials -- ferrous and non-ferrous metals, ceramics, composites, smart materials

units.title: Part B -- Manufacturing and Thermal Engineering
topics: Principles of casting, forming, joining and machining; introduction to CNC machines, 3D printing, smart manufacturing
Working principles of boilers, Otto and Diesel cycles, refrigeration and air-conditioning cycles, IC engines

units.title: Part B -- Power Plants, Transmission and Robotics
topics: Working principles of steam, diesel, hydro and nuclear power plants
Mechanical power transmission: belt, chain and rope drives, gear drives
Introduction to robotics -- joints, links, configurations and applications

course_outcomes: Understand the different manufacturing processes
Explain the basics of thermal engineering and its applications
Describe the working of different mechanical power transmission systems and power plants
Describe the basics of robotics and its applications

seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 1-2 Basic Civil and Mechanical Engineering notes: civil engineering basics, surveying, transportation and water resources, plus mechanical...

Detailed unit topics are sourced from an autonomous JNTUK-affiliated college's published syllabus and have not been independently verified against the university's own document -- autonomous colleges may adapt unit-level content locally.

subjects.notes: This combined Basic Civil Engineering (Part A) + Basic Mechanical Engineering (Part B) course appears identically in both the 1-1 and 1-2 source documents with no explicit L-T-P-C table row, official combined course title, or branch/semester-group scope statement in either -- unlike every other subject in this source, which states its branch scope directly. JNTUK R23 is known to swap this course with Basic Electrical & Electronics Engineering between semesters depending on branch group, but this source does not disambiguate which group takes it in 1-1 vs 1-2. Placed at 1-2 here (arbitrary pick pending verification); credits left null. Needs human verification against a branch-specific timetable before promoting to verified.

subjects.name: Differential Equations and Vector Calculus

units.title: Differential Equations of First Order and First Degree
topics: Linear differential equations, Bernoulli's equations, exact equations and equations reducible to exact form
Applications: Newton's law of cooling, law of natural growth and decay, electrical circuits (L-R circuits)

units.title: Linear Differential Equations of Higher Order with Constant Coefficients
topics: Homogeneous and non-homogeneous differential equations of higher order with constant coefficients
Method of variation of parameters, simultaneous linear equations
Applications: LCR circuit, simple harmonic motion

units.title: Partial Differential Equations
topics: Formation of partial differential equations by elimination of arbitrary constants and functions
Solutions of first-order linear equations using Lagrange's method
Higher-order homogeneous and non-homogeneous linear partial differential equations with constant coefficients

units.title: Vector Differentiation
topics: Scalar and vector point functions, vector operator del, gradient, directional derivative
Divergence (solenoidal), curl (irrotational), scalar potential, vector identities

units.title: Vector Integration
topics: Line integration, circulation, work done, surface integral, volume integral
Green's theorem in the plane, Stokes's theorem, divergence theorem (without proofs) and related problems

course_outcomes: Solve differential equations related to various engineering fields
Identify solution methods for partial differential equations that model physical processes
Interpret the physical meaning of operators such as gradient, curl and divergence
Estimate work done against a field, circulation and flux using vector calculus

seo.title: Differential Equations and Vector Calculus - 1-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 1-2 Differential Equations and Vector Calculus notes: first- and higher-order ODEs, PDEs, vector differentiation and vector integration.

Detailed unit topics are sourced from an autonomous JNTUK-affiliated college's published syllabus and have not been independently verified against the university's own document -- autonomous colleges may adapt unit-level content locally.

subjects.name: Engineering Physics

units.title: Wave Optics
topics: Interference: principle of superposition, interference in thin films (reflection geometry), colours in thin films
Newton's rings, determination of wavelength and refractive index
Diffraction: Fresnel and Fraunhofer diffraction, single/double/N-slit diffraction (qualitative), diffraction grating
Polarization: types of polarization, polarization by reflection/refraction/double refraction, Nicol prism, wave plates

units.title: Crystallography and X-ray Diffraction
topics: Space lattice, basis, unit cell and lattice parameters, Bravais lattices, crystal systems (3D)
Coordination number, packing fraction of SC, BCC and FCC, Miller indices, interplanar spacing
X-ray diffraction: Bragg's law, X-ray diffractometer, crystal structure determination by Laue's and powder methods

units.title: Dielectric and Magnetic Materials
topics: Dielectric polarization, polarizability, susceptibility, dielectric constant and displacement vector
Electronic, ionic and orientation polarization; Lorentz internal field, Clausius-Mossotti equation
Magnetic dipole moment, magnetization, susceptibility and permeability, classification of magnetic materials
Domain concept for ferromagnetism, hysteresis, soft and hard magnetic materials

units.title: Quantum Mechanics and Free Electron Theory
topics: Dual nature of matter, Heisenberg's uncertainty principle, significance and properties of the wave function
Schrodinger's time-independent and time-dependent wave equations, particle in a one-dimensional infinite potential well
Classical and quantum free electron theory, electrical conductivity, Fermi-Dirac distribution, density of states, Fermi energy

units.title: Semiconductors
topics: Formation of energy bands, classification of crystalline solids
Intrinsic and extrinsic semiconductors -- density of charge carriers, electrical conductivity, Fermi level
Drift and diffusion currents, Einstein's equation, Hall effect and its applications

course_outcomes: Analyze the intensity variation of light due to polarization, interference and diffraction
Familiarize with the basics of crystals and their structures
Explain fundamentals of quantum mechanics and apply them to one-dimensional particle motion
Summarize various types of dielectric polarization and classify magnetic materials
Identify the type of semiconductor using the Hall effect

seo.title: Engineering Physics - 1-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 CSE/EEE 1-2 Engineering Physics notes: wave optics, crystallography, dielectric and magnetic materials, quantum mechanics, and semiconductors.

source.college_source_note: Subject names, credits, and semester placement are cross-confirmed against JNTUK's own centrally-administered exam records. Detailed unit topics are sourced from an autonomous JNTUK-affiliated college's published syllabus and have not been independently verified against the university's own document -- autonomous colleges may adapt unit-level content locally.

subjects.name: Engineering Physics Lab

units.title: Laboratory Experiments
topics: Determine the radius of curvature of a plano-convex lens using Newton's rings
Determine wavelengths of mercury spectral lines using a diffraction grating at normal incidence
Verify Brewster's law
Determine dielectric constant using the charging and discharging method
Study the B-H curve of a magnetic material
Determine the wavelength of laser light using a diffraction grating
Estimate Planck's constant using the photoelectric effect
Determine semiconductor resistivity using the four-probe method
Determine the energy gap of a semiconductor using a PN-junction diode
Measure magnetic field along the axis of a current-carrying circular coil using Stewart and Gee's method
Determine Hall voltage and Hall coefficient of a semiconductor
Determine the temperature coefficient of a thermistor
Determine acceleration due to gravity and radius of gyration using a compound pendulum
Determine magnetic susceptibility using Kundt's tube
Determine rigidity modulus using a torsional pendulum
Verify the laws of a stretched string using a sonometer
Determine Young's modulus by non-uniform bending
Determine the frequency of an electrically maintained tuning fork using Melde's experiment

course_outcomes: Operate optical instruments such as travelling microscopes and spectrometers
Estimate wavelengths of light using a diffraction grating
Plot magnetic-field intensity along the axis of a current-carrying circular coil
Evaluate dielectric constant and magnetic susceptibility of materials
Calculate the band gap of a semiconductor
Identify semiconductor type using the Hall effect

seo.title: Engineering Physics Lab - 1-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 1-2 Engineering Physics Lab: optics, semiconductor and energy-gap experiments verifying interference, diffraction and Hall effect concepts.

source.college_source_note: Subject names, credits, and semester placement are cross-confirmed against JNTUK's own centrally-administered exam records. Detailed unit topics are sourced from an autonomous JNTUK-affiliated college's published syllabus and have not been independently verified against the university's own document -- autonomous colleges may adapt unit-level content locally.

subjects.name: Data Structures

units.title: Introduction to Linear Data Structures
topics: Definition and importance of linear data structures, abstract data types (ADTs)
Stack ADT, queue ADT, time and space complexity analysis for linear data structures
Searching: linear search, binary search
Sorting: bubble sort, selection sort, insertion sort, merge sort, quick sort

units.title: Linked Lists
topics: Singly linked lists: representation and operations
Doubly linked lists and circular linked lists
Comparing arrays and linked lists, applications of linked lists

units.title: Stacks
topics: Properties and operations, implementing stacks using arrays and linked lists
Applications in expression evaluation, backtracking, reversing a list, factorial calculation
Expression conversions: infix to postfix, infix to prefix, evaluating postfix expressions

units.title: Queues
topics: Properties and operations, implementing queues using arrays and linked lists
Applications in breadth-first search and scheduling
Circular queues, priority queues, deques

units.title: Trees and Graphs
topics: Introduction to trees, basic terminology, representation using arrays and linked lists
Binary search tree -- insertion, deletion and tree traversals
Introduction to graphs, graph traversals (BFS and DFS)

course_outcomes: Explain the role of linear data structures in organizing and accessing data efficiently
Design, implement, and apply linked lists for dynamic data storage
Develop programs using stacks to handle recursive algorithms and manage program state
Apply queue-based algorithms for task scheduling and graph traversal
Devise solutions to programming challenges involving stacks, queues and trees
Recognize scenarios where hashing is advantageous and design hash-based solutions

seo.title: Data Structures - JNTUK R23 1-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 CSE/IT 1-2 Data Structures: searching, sorting, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees and graph traversal.

source.college_source_note: Branch scope, credits, units, outcomes and lab experiments are stated in the published R23 first-year syllabus linked here; this copy is hosted by a JNTUK-affiliated college.

subjects.name: Data Structures Lab

units.title: Programming Exercises
topics: Reverse arrays; implement linear and binary search; implement bubble, selection and insertion sorting
Implement singly, doubly and circular linked lists with insertion, deletion, traversal and reversal
Use linked lists to remove duplicates and represent and add polynomials; implement a double-ended queue
Implement stacks using arrays and linked lists; evaluate postfix expressions and check balanced parentheses
Implement linear and circular queues; simulate a printer queue; use stacks and queues for expression conversion and symmetry checks
Implement and traverse a binary search tree

course_outcomes: Explain how linear data structures organize and provide efficient access to data
Design and implement linked lists for dynamic data storage
Develop stack-based programs for recursive algorithms and program-state management
Apply queues, deques and priority queues to scheduling, traversal and data-management problems
Recognize suitable uses for hashing and design hash-based solutions

seo.title: Data Structures Lab - JNTUK R23 1-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 CSE/IT 1-2 Data Structures Lab: arrays, searching, sorting, linked lists, stacks, queues and binary search trees.

source.college_source_note: Branch scope, credits, units, outcomes and lab experiments are stated in the published R23 first-year syllabus linked here; this copy is hosted by a JNTUK-affiliated college.

subjects.name: Chemistry

units.title: Structure and Bonding Models
topics: Fundamentals of quantum mechanics, Schrodinger wave equation, significance of psi and psi-squared
Particle in a one-dimensional box
Molecular orbital theory -- bonding in homo- and hetero-nuclear diatomic molecules, energy level diagrams of O2 and CO
Pi-molecular orbitals of butadiene and benzene, calculation of bond order

units.title: Modern Engineering Materials
topics: Semiconductors -- introduction, basic concepts, applications
Superconductors -- introduction, basic concepts, applications
Supercapacitors -- introduction, basic concepts, classification, applications
Nanomaterials -- introduction, classification, properties and applications of fullerenes, carbon nanotubes and graphene nanoparticles

units.title: Electrochemistry and Applications
topics: Electrochemical cell, Nernst equation, cell potential calculations
Potentiometry -- potentiometric titrations (redox); conductivity, conductometric titrations (acid-base)
Construction and working of standard hydrogen electrode and calomel electrode
Primary cells (zinc-air battery), secondary cells (lithium-ion batteries) -- working and cell reactions
Fuel cells, hydrogen-oxygen fuel cell, polymer electrolyte membrane fuel cells (PEMFC)

units.title: Polymer Chemistry
topics: Introduction to polymers, functionality of monomers, chain growth and step growth polymerization
Coordination polymerization with examples and mechanisms
Thermoplastics and thermosetting plastics -- PVC, Teflon, Bakelite, Nylon-6,6, carbon fibres
Elastomers -- Buna-S, Buna-N: preparation, properties and applications
Conducting polymers -- polyacetylene, polyaniline; biodegradable polymers -- PGA, PLA

units.title: Instrumental Methods and Applications
topics: Electromagnetic spectrum, absorption of radiation, Beer-Lambert law -- applications and limitations
UV-visible spectroscopy -- electronic transitions and instrumentation
IR spectroscopy -- instrumentation, identification and applications
Procedure and applications of MRI and CT scans

course_outcomes: Understand the concepts of molecular orbitals and bond orders
Apply the principle of band diagrams in the application of conductors and semiconductors
Compare the materials of construction for battery and fuel cells
Explain the preparation, properties, and applications of thermoplastics, thermosetting polymers, elastomers, and conducting polymers
Summarize the concepts of instrumental methods and explain the principles of spectrometry

seo.title: Chemistry - JNTUK R23 1-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 ECE/IT 1-2 Chemistry: bonding models, engineering materials, electrochemistry, polymers and instrumental analysis.

source.college_source_note: Branch scope, credits, units, outcomes and lab experiments are stated in the published R23 first-year syllabus linked here; this copy is hosted by a JNTUK-affiliated college.

subjects.name: Chemistry Lab

units.title: Laboratory Experiments
topics: Measure 10Dq by spectrophotometry; verify the Beer-Lambert law; measure sample wavelength using UV-visible spectroscopy
Conductometric titrations of strong and weak acids against a strong base; determine cell constant and solution conductance
Use potentiometry to determine redox potentials and EMF; determine acid strength in a lead-acid battery
Prepare Bakelite and nanomaterials by precipitation; identify simple organic compounds using IR spectroscopy
Estimate ferrous iron, total water hardness, vitamin C and magnesium in an antacid

course_outcomes: Determine the cell constant and conductance of solutions
Prepare advanced polymer Bakelite materials
Measure the strength of an acid present in secondary batteries
Analyse the IR spectra of some organic compounds
Calculate the strength of acid in a Pb-acid battery

seo.title: Chemistry Lab - JNTUK R23 1-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 ECE/IT 1-2 Chemistry Lab: conductivity, potentiometry, polymers, spectroscopy, nanomaterials, hardness and vitamin C experiments.

source.college_source_note: Branch scope, credits, units, outcomes and lab experiments are stated in the published R23 first-year syllabus linked here; this copy is hosted by a JNTUK-affiliated college.

subjects.name: Network Analysis

units.title: Circuit Analysis Fundamentals
topics: Types of circuit components, types of sources and source transformations
Mesh and nodal analysis with dependent sources, principle of duality
Steady-state analysis of AC circuits: impedance concept, phase angle, series R-L, R-C, R-L-C circuits
Complex impedance and phasor notation, star-delta conversion

units.title: Resonance and Coupled Circuits
topics: Series resonance, bandwidth, parallel resonance, anti-resonance
Coupled circuits: self-inductance, mutual inductance, coefficient of coupling
Dot rule of coupled circuits, conductively coupled equivalent circuits

units.title: Network Theorems
topics: Thevenin's, Norton's, Millman's, reciprocity, compensation, substitution, superposition, maximum power transfer, and Tellegen's theorems
Problem solving using dependent sources under AC and DC excitation

units.title: Transients and Laplace Transform
topics: First-order differential equations, time constants, R-L and R-C circuits with DC excitation
Second-order differential equations (homogeneous and non-homogeneous) with R-L-C elements
Laplace transform: basic theorems, partial fraction expansion, Heaviside expansion

units.title: Two-Port Networks
topics: Z-parameters, Y-parameters, transmission-line parameters, h-parameters, relationships between parameter sets
Parallel and series connection, cascading of two-port networks
Image and iterative impedances, insertion loss, attenuators, lattice networks, impedance matching

course_outcomes: Understand basic electrical circuits with nodal and mesh analysis
Analyze circuits using network simplification theorems
Find transient and steady-state response of a network
Analyze electrical networks in the Laplace domain
Compute the parameters of a two-port network

seo.title: Network Analysis - ECE 1-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 ECE 1-2 Network Analysis notes: circuit fundamentals, resonance, network theorems, transients and Laplace transform, and two-port networks.

subjects.notes: This source PDF did not include an explicit L-T-P-C table row for this subject (unlike its EEE/CSE-IT counterparts); credits are inferred by pattern-matching against Electrical Circuit Analysis-I and Data Structures, both 3-0-0-3 in the same regulation and year -- needs confirmation against an explicit source before treating the credit value as verified.

subjects.name: Network Analysis Lab

units.title: Network Analysis Experiments
topics: Study circuit components and verify Kirchhoff's current and voltage laws
Verify mesh and nodal analysis for AC circuits
Determine Q factor and bandwidth of series- and parallel-resonance circuits
Verify superposition, Thevenin and Norton theorems for AC circuits
Verify the maximum-power-transfer theorem for AC circuits
Verify Tellegen's theorem for two networks with the same topology
Study DC transients in RL, RC and RLC circuits
Study frequency response of first-order RL and RC networks
Study transient and steady-state responses of a second-order circuit as its parameters vary
Determine open-circuit Z and short-circuit Y parameters
Determine hybrid H and transmission ABCD parameters
Measure two-port parameters and frequency response of a twin-T network

course_outcomes: Verify Kirchhoff's laws and network theorems
Measure time constants of RL and RC circuits
Analyze RLC-circuit behaviour under different conditions
Design a resonant circuit for given specifications
Characterize a two-port network using standard network parameters

seo.title: Network Analysis Lab - ECE 1-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 ECE 1-2 Network Analysis Lab: verification of Kirchoff’s laws, network theorems, resonance and two-port network parameters.

Detailed unit topics are sourced from an autonomous JNTUK-affiliated college's published syllabus and have not been independently verified against the university's own document -- autonomous colleges may adapt unit-level content locally.

subjects.name: Electrical Circuit Analysis-I

units.title: Introduction to Electrical Circuits
topics: Passive elements R, L, C and their V-I relations; dependent and independent sources
Kirchoff's laws, network reduction techniques (series, parallel, series-parallel, star-delta and delta-star transformation)
Source transformation, nodal and mesh analysis of DC networks with dependent and independent sources

units.title: Magnetic Circuits
topics: MMF, flux and reluctance, analogy between electrical and magnetic circuits
Faraday's laws of electromagnetic induction, self and mutual inductance
Dot convention, coefficient of coupling, analysis of series and parallel magnetic circuits

units.title: Single Phase Circuits
topics: Characteristics of periodic functions, average value, RMS value, form factor, phasor representation
Steady-state analysis of R, L, C circuits to sinusoidal excitation
Series and parallel RL, RC, RLC circuit response

units.title: Resonance and Locus Diagrams
topics: Series resonance: Q-factor, selectivity, bandwidth, half-power frequencies
Parallel resonance: Q-factor, selectivity, bandwidth
Locus diagrams: RL, RC, RLC with R, L and C variables

units.title: Network Theorems (DC and AC Excitations)
topics: Superposition theorem, Thevenin's theorem, Norton's theorem
Maximum power transfer theorem, reciprocity theorem, Millman's theorem, compensation theorem

course_outcomes: Remember the basic electrical elements and fundamental laws
Understand network reduction techniques, self/mutual inductance, phasor diagrams, resonance and network theorems
Apply concepts to obtain mathematical and graphical representations
Analyze nodal and mesh networks, series and parallel circuits, and steady-state response
Evaluate network theorems and electrical, magnetic and single-phase circuits

seo.title: Electrical Circuit Analysis-I - EEE 1-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 EEE 1-2 Electrical Circuit Analysis-I notes: circuit fundamentals, magnetic circuits, single-phase circuits, resonance and network theorems.

subjects.name: Electrical Circuits Lab

units.title: Electrical Circuits Experiments
topics: Verify Kirchhoff's circuit laws
Verify node and mesh analysis
Verify network-reduction techniques
Determine the cold and hot resistance of an electric lamp
Determine the parameters of a choke coil
Determine self-inductance, mutual inductance and coefficient of coupling
Study series and parallel resonance
Plot locus diagrams of RL and RC series circuits
Verify the superposition theorem
Verify Thevenin's and Norton's theorems
Verify the maximum-power-transfer theorem
Verify the compensation theorem
Verify reciprocity and Millman's theorems

course_outcomes: Explain network theorems, node and mesh analysis, resonance and locus diagrams
Apply network theorems and compare practical results with theoretical calculations
Determine choke-coil parameters, self- and mutual inductance and coefficient of coupling
Analyze circuit characteristics using fundamental laws and network configurations
Create locus diagrams and examine series and parallel resonance

seo.title: Electrical Circuits Lab - EEE 1-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 EEE 1-2 Electrical Circuits Lab: verification of network theorems, resonance, and locus diagrams through hands-on circuit experiments.
